title: eas-contracts (README)
quote: The Ethereum Attestation Service is a free and open protocol for on-chain attestations on EVM compatible blockchains.

title: eas-contracts – Mainnet deployment (v0.26)
quote: Ethereum Mainnet (v0.26): EAS 0xA1207F3BBa224E2c9c3c6D5aF63D0eb1582Ce587, SchemaRegistry 0xA7b39296258348C78294F95B872b282326A97BDF.

title: EAS Attestation struct (Common.sol)
quote: struct Attestation { bytes32 uid; bytes32 schema; uint64 time; uint64 expirationTime; uint64 revocationTime; bytes32 refUID; address recipient; address attester; bool revocable; bytes data; }

title: ISchemaRegistry interface
quote: struct SchemaRecord { bytes32 uid; ISchemaResolver resolver; bool revocable; string schema; } function register(string calldata schema, ISchemaResolver resolver, bool revocable) external returns (bytes32);

title: SchemaResolver abstract contract
quote: function attest(Attestation calldata attestation) external payable onlyEAS returns (bool); function revoke(Attestation calldata attestation) external payable onlyEAS returns (bool);

title: What Is Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS) & How to Use It
quote: EAS leverages EIP-712 for signing, ensuring that attestations are secure and verifiable.

title: Contracts (EAS) – Optimism Docs
quote: The Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS) is an open-source public good included as a predeploy in the OP Stack.

title: Coinbase Verifications – README
quote: 'Coinbase Verifications' is a set of Coinbase-verified onchain attestations that enable access to apps and other onchain benefits.

title: Coinbase Verifications – schema IDs / attester
quote: Base Mainnet Verified Account schema 0xf8b05c...0de9, Verified Country schema 0x180190...a065, Coinbase Attester 0x357458739F90461b99789350868CD7CF330Dd7EE.

title: Coinbase rolls out on-chain credential verification using EAS
quote: Verifications are issued through the Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS)… on Ethereum and its layer-2 network Base.

title: Gitcoin Passport – Smart contract overview
quote: Gitcoin Passport creates a Stamp and score attestation, and mints them onchain to EAS and other attestation registries, depending on which network users choose.

title: EAS Docs – Delegated Attestations
quote: Delegated Attestations enable an entity to sign an attestation while allowing another entity to cover the transaction fee.

title: EAS Docs – GraphQL API
quote: The Easscan GraphQL API grants access to attestation and schema information across multiple blockchain networks (Ethereum, Optimism, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Scroll, Linea, Celo, …).

title: Verifiable Credentials Data Model
quote: A verifiable credential is a tamper-evident credential whose authorship can be cryptographically verified.

title: Soulbound – Vitalik Buterin
quote: A soulbound item, once picked up, cannot be transferred or sold to another player.

title: EIP-5192: Minimal Soulbound NFTs
quote: This standard is an extension of EIP-721. It proposes a minimal interface to make tokens soulbound using the feature detection functionality of EIP-165.

title: Introducing Verax, An On-Chain Attestation Registry
quote: Verax is not an Ethereum Improvement Proposal (EIP), or a protocol, or a product, but rather a simple primitive.

title: Sign Protocol documentation
quote: Sign Protocol is the evidence layer used across sovereign and institutional workloads.

title: EAS Ecosystem page
quote: 420k+ unique attestors; integrations include Optimism Bedrock, Coinbase Verifications, Gitcoin Passport, Guild, Icebreaker, KarmaHQ, Scroll Canvas, Arbitrum Arcade, Ceramic.

title: Optimism to Use Ethereum Attestation Service to Promote User Trust
quote: In May 2023, Optimism began using Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS), an on-chain attestation protocol.

title: EAS Delegated Attestations – proxy / nonce
quote: Standard delegated attestations require an incrementing nonce based on the EIP-712 spec, but the proxy contract allows you to accept delegated attestations out of order and with an expiration time.

title: EAS SDK – off-chain attestation signing
quote: const offchain = await eas.getOffchain(); const offchainAttestation = await offchain.signOffchainAttestation({...}, signer);

title: Attestations: Sign Everything Onchain
quote: SIGN is an omni-chain attestation protocol designed to enable users to freely attest and verify any information on-chain.

title: Decentralized Society: Finding Web3's Soul
quote: Non-transferable 'soulbound' tokens (SBTs) representing the commitments, credentials, and affiliations of 'Souls' can encode the trust networks of the real economy to establish provenance and reputation.

title: easscan.org – Ethereum mainnet explorer
quote: Total Attestations: 13,730; Total Schemas: 372; Unique Attestors: 781 (Ethereum mainnet, at access time).

title: Coinbase Verifications – Verified Pools Dev Docs
quote: For Verified Account attestations, the data field is a single boolean that is always true; for Verified Country attestations, the data field contains the ISO country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).

title: EAS Docs – FAQs (audit & revocation)
quote: EAS contracts have undergone a thorough audit by Spearbit, a reputable third-party firm. Attestations are immutable… EAS allows the issuer of the attestation to revoke the onchain or offchain attestation. It does not delete the attestation.
